meta.title: The Cow and the Tiger — Set 2
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Vocabulary quiz for 'The Cow and the Tiger' — key words and meanings.

questions.question: What does 'beautiful' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful' means very pretty or lovely. Bala 'had a beautiful calf' — her baby was very lovely.

questions.question: 'Angry' means very upset or mad.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Angry' means very upset or mad. The tiger was 'angry' when he caught Bala — he was mad and ready to attack.

questions.question: The tiger caught Bala because he was very _______.
questions.hint: The opposite of calm; very upset
questions.explanation: 'Angry' means very upset or mad. The tiger was angry when he caught Bala in the forest.

questions.question: What does 'attack' mean?
options: To suddenly hurt or try to hurt someone
questions.explanation: 'Attack' means to suddenly try to hurt someone. Bala asked the tiger not to attack her — not to try to hurt her.

questions.question: What does 'sad' mean?
options: Feeling unhappy or full of sorrow
questions.explanation: 'Sad' means feeling unhappy or full of sorrow. 'Sad Bala cried' — she was very unhappy when the tiger caught her.

questions.question: Tiger _______ to let Bala go. He said yes to her request.
questions.explanation: 'Agreed' means said yes or accepted. The tiger 'agreed' — he said yes and let Bala go to feed her calf.

questions.question: 'Grassy' means covered in grass.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Grassy' means covered with grass. Bala's home was in a 'grassy' area — an open field with lots of grass.

questions.question: The tiger was 'surprised' when Bala came back. What does 'surprised' mean?
options: Feeling shocked or amazed by something unexpected
Feeling tired after a long wait
questions.explanation: 'Surprised' means feeling shocked or amazed by something unexpected. The tiger did not expect Bala to come back — so he was very surprised.

questions.question: Sort these words — are they describing FEELINGS or describing PLACES/SETTINGS?
questions.explanation: 'Angry,' 'surprised,' and 'happy' describe feelings. 'Shadowy' (full of shadows) and 'grassy' (covered in grass) describe places in the story.

questions.question: The tiger decided to _______ Bala from other animals — he kept her safe.
questions.hint: To keep someone safe from danger
questions.explanation: 'Protect' means to keep someone safe. The tiger promised to 'protect Bala from other animals' at the end of the story.

questions.question: Bala told the tiger the _______ — she really came back just as she said.
questions.explanation: 'Truth' means what is real and honest. The tiger said 'You told the truth' because Bala really did come back as she promised.

questions.question: 'Named' is a verb — it means being called by a name.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Named' is a verb meaning 'called by a name.' The story begins 'Once there was a cow named Bala' — her name was Bala.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Tiger was pleased that Bala kept her word.' Keeping her word means doing what she promised.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'beautiful' correctly?
options: The tiger was beautiful to catch Bala.
Bala had a beautiful calf with soft, lovely fur.
Bala graze beautiful in the forest.
questions.explanation: 'Beautiful' is an adjective used to describe the calf. 'Bala had a beautiful calf' — the calf was lovely. This is the correct use of the word.

questions.question: Sort the vocabulary from the story by their part of speech.
questions.explanation: 'Promise' and 'truth' are things (nouns). 'Protect' is an action (verb). 'Shadowy' and 'grassy' describe how things look (adjectives).
